summary: Microsoft documents disabling low disk space checks through the NoLowDiskSpaceChecks registry value under HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer. The 25H2 raw registry dump lists the same value name under the machine Explorer policy branch, and the app implementation was aligned to that documented machine-policy path on 2026-03-13. This record therefore validates the official control surface rather than the previously mismatched HKCU variant.

setting.name: Low Disk Space Warning
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can warn you when a drive is running low on space. People sometimes turn these warnings off on lab machines or kiosk-like systems, but most users should keep them on.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable low disk warnings when they already monitor storage another way, when alerts are noisy on managed systems, or when they are working with temporary lab environments.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning the warning off reduces notification noise, but it also removes a useful warning that helps many users avoid running a drive completely full.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents NoLowDiskSpaceChecks in an official troubleshooting article using HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer with value 1. The 25H2 raw registry dump lists NoLowDiskSpaceChecks under the same machine Explorer policy branch, which corroborates that the value family is still present on current builds. The app previously wrote HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\NoLowDiskSpaceChecks = 1, but that implementation mismatch was corrected on 2026-03-13 to use the documented HKLM path. The official article is the primary proof source and names the exact key path, value name, and value data.

allowed_values.label: Windows default
allowed_values.meaning: Windows follows its normal low disk warning behavior and can display low-disk warnings.

allowed_values.label: Low disk warning disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not check for or display the documented low disk space warnings.

targets.notes: Windows default is missing (key absent). The official article documents writing 1 to disable the warning; deleting the value returns the system to normal warning behavior.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app now writes HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\NoLowDiskSpaceChecks = 1, which matches the documented Microsoft control surface. The older HKCU variant was removed on 2026-03-13 as part of the implementation-mismatch fix.

windows_defaults.label: Windows documented baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems using the documented Explorer machine-policy path

states.rationale: Leave the documented registry value absent.

windows_defaults.notes: This record uses only the documented HKLM path from the official Microsoft article.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Anyone who wants Windows to keep warning about low disk space
avoid_for: Managed systems that intentionally suppress storage alerts
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep warning users when storage is running low.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You already manage low storage conditions another way and want fewer alerts.
tradeoffs: Windows can continue surfacing helpful low-disk warnings.
No registry value is written at the documented policy path.

recommended_profiles.label: Disable low disk space warning
intended_for: Managed systems that intentionally suppress low storage alerts
Kiosk or lab environments monitored another way
avoid_for: General users who rely on Windows to warn before storage is exhausted

states.rationale: Use the documented Explorer machine-policy value from the Microsoft article.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You already manage storage pressure another way and want to suppress this specific Windows warning.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Windows to keep warning users when disks are running low on space.
tradeoffs: Windows stops checking for or displaying the documented low-disk warnings.
Users may miss an early warning before a drive becomes full.
recommended_profiles.notes: apply_allowed=true. The proof chain is exact: the Microsoft article names the HKLM path, NoLowDiskSpaceChecks value, and value data 1. The app now writes the same machine-policy path.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn troubleshooting article for low disk space warnings
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents NoLowDiskSpaceChecks under HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer with value 1 to turn off low disk space checks and warnings.

evidence.title: 25H2 raw registry corroboration for NoLowDiskSpaceChecks
evidence.summary: The 25H2 raw registry dump lists NoLowDiskSpaceChecks under the machine Explorer policy branch, corroborating that the value family is still present on current builds.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\NoLowDiskSpaceChecks = 1 for the current tweak.

decision.why: The Microsoft troubleshooting article documents the exact HKLM Explorer path, NoLowDiskSpaceChecks value name, and value data 1. The 25H2 raw registry dump corroborates that the value family is still present on current builds. The app now writes the same machine-policy path, so the implementation matches the primary source and the record can be validated. recommended_for_general_users stays false because suppressing low-disk warnings is a niche administrative choice rather than a broadly helpful default.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Locate and select the following registry subkey: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer. Enter NoLowDiskSpaceChecks and press Enter. Type 1 in the Value data box and select OK.
validation_proof.notes: The official troubleshooting article documents the HKLM Explorer policy path and NoLowDiskSpaceChecks = 1 flow exactly. The app now writes the same machine-policy path.
